name: Shivam Bhardwaj
title: I build and run wet labs at biotech startups.
bio:
Molecular biologist with 10+ years from PhD-level bench work to running BSL-2 facilities for 100+ scientists. Most recently sole bench scientist at Exonic (YC F25), an AI-driven gene therapy startup, where I designed and ran a two-wave MPRA pipeline to validate AI-generated synthetic enhancer constructs across human and mouse cell lines.

I'm at my best at the seed-to-Series-A stage — where the same person needs to design the experiments, build the lab, write the SOPs, and call the vendors. Currently looking for the right founding scientist, scientific lead, or cofounder role at an early-stage biotech in the Bay Area.

experience.dept: AI-driven Gene Therapy Startup · San Francisco
bullets: Sole bench scientist at an AI-driven gene therapy startup.Built high-throughput cloning, quantitative assay development, and cell line infrastructure from the ground up.
Identified and characterized tissue-selective activity for top-performing CRE constructs across CNS and muscle lineages.
Designed a two-wave massively parallel reporter assay (MPRA) pipeline to validate AI-generated synthetic enhancer constructs across human and mouse cell lines.
Partnered with a top AI research lab to advance model reasoning within life sciences and specialized research domains.
Authored high-complexity SFT datasets — adversarial prompts and multi-step reasoning problems with verified ground-truth solutions.
Consistently exceeded benchmarks on accuracy and volume in large-scale data labeling for LLM training pipelines.

experience.role: Program Manager / Research Infrastructure Supplier Manager
experience.dept: Research Platforms | Research Management & Innovation Directorate (RMID)
bullets: Co-led a $5M+ annual research infrastructure fund, overseeing strategic planning, contract management, and procurement of all scientific equipment and services.
Developed data-driven procurement strategies using PowerBI, optimizing purchasing processes and saving over $2.2 million in a single year.
Led cross-functional projects with academics, suppliers, regulatory bodies, and estates teams to deliver complex facility upgrades within tight schedules.
Managed daily lab operations for 100+ scientists across BSL-2 molecular biology, chemistry, tissue culture, histology, and radioisotope labs in a preclinical cancer research center.

Certified Biological Safety Supervisor and Radiation Protection Advisor; established all EHS programs (Chemical Hygiene Plan, BBP, hazardous waste), ensuring audit-readiness against UK/EU/USA protocols.
Authored, validated, and implemented SOPs for core facility equipment and complex assays. Inducted and trained lab users on multiple techniques.
Implemented LIMS (FreezerPro) for 10,000+ human tissue and blood samples, ensuring data integrity and long-term storage.

experience.role: Staff Research Associate II, DNA Sequencing Core
bullets: Performed QC, data analysis (demultiplexing, de novo assembly), and troubleshooting for diverse sequencing projects.
Consulted dozens of scientists on experimental design, sample prep, genotyping, and sequencing strategies.

experience.org: Counterman Lab — Auburn University & Mississippi State University
bullets: Managed 3 research projects in an NSF-funded R1 lab, including co-leading design, setup, and launch of the new lab from scratch.
Built foundational protocols and imaging workflow systems.
Led CRISPR-Cas9 knockout protocols in 3 butterfly species; created transcriptomics and bioinformatics pipelines.

education.detail:
Dept. of Biological Sciences. Advisor: Prof. Antónia Monteiro.
Thesis: The Evolution of Phenotypic Plasticity in Butterflies (wing pattern evolution, phenotypic plasticity, sexual dimorphism and evo-devo questions across 13 species).

education.school: Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER Mohali), India
education.detail:
Major: Biology. Minor: Chemistry, Mathematics and Physics.
MS Thesis: Identification of novel genes for long term memory formation in C. elegans using ChIP sequencing. Advisor: Prof. Kavita Babu.
